title: Chopper RunResult v1
description: Wire-format schema for the summary object emitted by ChopperRunner.run(ctx). This is the 'outer' payload a future GUI or CI tool consumes — it is intentionally compact. The full manifest and dependency graph live in their own audit artifacts under `.chopper/` (compiled_manifest.json, dependency_graph.json); this schema pins only the summary surface so that downstream tooling can render a run outcome without deserializing megabytes of manifest data. Authoritative field definitions live in ARCHITECTURE_PLAN.md §9.1 (RunResult, AuditManifest) and bible §5.5. This schema is the single contract between the CLI and any potential GUI, ensuring both can evolve independently while remaining compatible; CI validates every RunResult against this schema, so breaking changes surface immediately.

schema.description: Schema identifier. Mismatched consumers refuse to parse rather than drift silently.

run_id.description: Filesystem-safe ISO-8601 UTC start time, e.g. '2026-04-21T14-30-05Z'. Same run_id appears in every audit artifact produced by this invocation — see bible §5.5.12 (correlation).

exit_code.description: Process exit code per bible §5.9 / CLI_HELP_TEXT_REFERENCE.md. 0=success, 1=validation failures, 2=usage error, 3=internal/programmer error (an `.chopper/internal-error.log` is also written in this case).

started_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp with timezone (always Z). Same instant as run_id, in RFC-3339 form for downstream tools that want a real datetime.

ended_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp with timezone. Always >= started_at.

duration_seconds.description: Wall-clock seconds between started_at and ended_at. Convenience field so consumers don't re-parse the timestamps.

subcommand.description: Which CLI verb produced this result.

dry_run.description: True when invoked with `--dry-run`. No domain files were modified; only .chopper/ artifacts were written.

strict.description: True when invoked with `--strict`. Warnings forced the exit code to 1 (see DIAGNOSTIC_CODES.md exit-code policy).

root.description: Absolute POSIX path to the resolved domain root.

state.description: Domain lifecycle state detected by P0 (DomainStateService). See bible §2.8.

command.description: Selection inputs so a reviewer can reproduce the run.

mode.description: Which of the three input modes was used (bible §5.9).

base_json.description: Absolute POSIX path to the resolved base JSON.

feature_jsons.description: Ordered list of resolved feature JSON paths in CLI / project order.

project_json.description: Absolute POSIX path to the project JSON when --project was used; null otherwise.

project_notes.description: Verbatim `notes` array from project JSON, preserved for audit.

artifacts_present.description: Domain-relative POSIX names of files written under .chopper/ during this run, lex-sorted. Enumerated per bible §5.5.10.

manifest_summary.description: Compact counts from CompiledManifest. Null when P3 never produced a manifest (e.g. P1 aborted).

graph_summary.description: Compact counts from DependencyGraph. Null when P4 never ran.

pi_plus_nodes.description: Size of PI+ (reachable_from_includes). Reporting-only: PI+ nodes are NOT auto-copied; see bible §5.4.

trim_stats.description: Corresponds to trim_stats.json. Null for `validate` and `cleanup`.

top_codes.description: Top N most-frequent codes in this run, descending by count then lex-ascending by code.

internal_error.description: Populated only when exit_code == 3. Mirrors the top of .chopper/internal-error.log so that GUIs / CI can surface the programmer-error without parsing the log file.
